I.title: Registry as Single Source of Truth
I.rule: All repo state lives in repo-registry.json (in meta-organvm/organvm-corpvs-testamentvm). If reality and registry disagree, update the registry or fix reality.

II.title: Unidirectional Dependencies
II.rule: Flow is I→II→III only. No back-edges. ORGAN-IV orchestrates, ORGAN-V documents, ORGAN-VII amplifies.

III.title: All Eight Organs Visible at Launch
III.rule: Each organ has at least one representative at launch. The eight-organ system must be visible in its entirety.

IV.title: Documentation Precedes Deployment
IV.rule: No Phase N+1 until Phase N is complete. Documentation is the deliverable.

V.title: Portfolio-Quality Documentation
V.rule: Every README is a portfolio piece. The Stranger Test applies: a grant reviewer seeing this for the first time should be convinced.

VI.title: Promotion State Machine
VI.rule: Repos follow: LOCAL → CANDIDATE → PUBLIC_PROCESS → GRADUATED → ARCHIVED. No state may be skipped.

A.title: Bronze Tier Launch Path
A.rule: 5 Perfect Repos > 44 Mediocre Repos. Completion is criteria-driven, not time-boxed.

B.title: Coordination Overhead Budget
B.rule: Budget 10% of phase TE as explicit line item for reconciling parallel AI streams.

C.title: Registry Schema Completeness
C.rule: registry.json must include dependencies[], promotion_status, tier, and last_validated fields before Phase 2.

D.title: AI Non-Determinism Acknowledgment
D.rule: All AI-generated deliverables require human review. Budget estimates use scenario banding.

E.title: Session Lifecycle Mandate
E.rule: All development sessions follow the FRAME->SHAPE->BUILD->PROVE lifecycle. No implementation without a plan. See docs/conductor-playbook.md.

F.title: Agent Coordination Visibility
F.rule: All sessions leave a breadcrumb at completion using the standardized format (docs/breadcrumb-protocol.md). Non-interactive agents must comply with the safety protocol (petasum-super-petasum/docs/NON-INTERACTIVE-AGENT-SAFETY.md).

G.title: Per-PR Lifecycle (Score/Rehearse/Perform)
G.rule: All pull requests follow the Score/Rehearse/Perform lifecycle. Score defines scope and acceptance criteria in the issue. Rehearse iterates via FSBP sessions until green. Perform merges with governance checklist and postmortem. See docs/score-rehearse-perform.md.

H.title: The Conscious Reality (Temporal Manifestation)
H.rule: Human clock time is a translated projection only. The system primary indices follow the Triple Cycle hierarchy: Macro (Ecosystem Pulse), Meso (Session Step), and Micro (Token Density). See docs/temporal-manifestation.md.

registry.question: Does this deliverable update registry.json? Is the schema satisfied?

portfolio.question: Does this pass the Stranger Test? Score >=90/100 for flagships.

dependency.question: Does this respect I→II→III flow? No back-edges?

completeness.question: 0 TBD markers? 0 broken links? No placeholder content?

conditions: Source repo has comprehensive README (>=2000 words)

wip_limits.note: Aspirational ceilings. Enforcement is warn-only until backlog is resolved.

classified_governance._note: Wave 2 — rules that already exist as code/YAML/prose but were not in the threshold grid. Classified, not invented.

interface_read_many_write_one.title: Interface Read-Many-Write-One
interface_read_many_write_one.rule: Interface organs (V, VI, VII) observe all organs but write only to their own repos. ORGAN-V documents, ORGAN-VI convenes, ORGAN-VII distributes.

editorial_governance.title: Editorial Governance (ORGAN-V)
editorial_governance.rule: All published content governed by editorial-standards: frontmatter-schema.yaml, quality-rubric.yaml, tag-governance.yaml, category-taxonomy.yaml. Schema changes require an ADR.

frontmatter_schema_validation.title: Frontmatter Schema Validation
frontmatter_schema_validation.rule: Every essay must pass 11 required fields against frontmatter-schema.yaml. Unknown fields cause validation failure. Enforced by essay-pipeline/src/validator.py.

essay_file_constraints.title: Essay File-Level Constraints
essay_file_constraints.rule: Essay naming: YYYY-MM-DD-slug.md. Tags: lowercase-hyphenated from controlled vocabulary. word_count >= 500. excerpt: 50-400 chars. layout: must be 'essay'.

production_repo_conventions.title: Production Repo Conventions
production_repo_conventions.rule: Each production repo (I, II, III) must have a CLAUDE.md with build/test/lint commands, a seed.yaml contract, and follow its organ's stack conventions (Python: PEP 8, type hints, ruff; TypeScript: strict mode, named exports).

seed_schema_conformance.title: seed.yaml Schema Conformance
seed_schema_conformance.rule: Every seed.yaml must conform to seed/v1.0 schema: organ, organ_name, repo, org, metadata (implementation_status, tier, promotion_status, last_validated), produces, consumes.

action_ledger_emission.title: Action Ledger Self-Recording
action_ledger_emission.rule: Every state transition must self-record via emit_state_change() with origin: emitted. Actions carry subsystem, from_state, to_state params. The action ledger's record() is atomic: recording IS composing IS manifesting.

ci_workflow_enforcement.title: CI Workflow Enforcement
ci_workflow_enforcement.rule: 17 GitHub Actions workflows enforce atom-level governance: dependency validation, registry health audits, promotion gating, secret scanning, essay monitoring, content distribution, CodeQL analysis.

logic_first_governance.title: Logic-First Governance Principles
logic_first_governance.rule: Logic is the only self-justifying principle. All governance decisions resolve through formal reasoning. Contradictory principles must be resolved through the logic framework before enforcement. COMMANDMENTS.md is the philosophical substrate.

non_interactive_agent_safety.title: Non-Interactive Agent Safety Protocol
non_interactive_agent_safety.rule: Non-interactive agents must comply with safety constraints: bounded execution, explicit permission scopes, breadcrumb trails, no destructive operations without human gate.

organ_aesthetic_identity.title: Organ Visual Identity (organ-aesthetic.yaml)
organ_aesthetic_identity.rule: Each organ defines visual identity via organ-aesthetic.yaml: palette, typography, tone, layout patterns. Interface-facing repos must conform to their organ's aesthetic.
